summary: ISS-1486 (c4 H5, moderate) is already resolved by EXP-235 (ISS-1463, integrated in commit 969dce06 at 2026-04-23T14:33Z, roughly 55 minutes after the c4 review was done). EXP-235 added the δZ = baseline × coverage × FSF scaling equation, the FSF = H(r)/r dome-geometric ratio with Pearson r ≈ 0.9999, and the 2017 Boulder absolute-magnitude import to detail_evidence P4 — exactly the elements c4 H5 requires. Verified by substring checks against current data/wins.json WIN-025.detail_evidence: all four architectural anchors ("scaling equation", "FSF = H(r)/r", "Pearson", "2017 Boulder") are present verbatim. c4 H5 is a stale restatement of c3 H3 — the curmudgeon was reading pre-EXP-235 text. Recommended action: close ISS-1486 as duplicate / already-resolved, mark EXP-239 complete on integration. Zero text patches needed.
